rationale.R1.1: Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' is an explicit directive to reconstruct the thesis and contribution hierarchy. The artifact contains two competing candidate primary contributions (the DiD causal estimate vs. CrowdAudit) plus a sweeping normative conclusion ('wholesale revision of the platform-labor literature') that may not follow from the reported effect size; the reframe must adjudicate which contribution anchors the paper and whether the gap statement survives.
rationale.R1.2: Central: any reframing must preserve or rebuild the logical chain from identification strategy through treatment-effect estimate through normative call-to-action. The current pivot from causal inference to detection methodology mid-paper creates a consistency pressure that the reframe must resolve.
rationale.R1.4: Central: the conceptual story includes a novelty claim that currently straddles two distinct contributions (causal identification and detection tool). Reframing requires repositioning the paper's delta against prior causal-inference work on crowdwork quality and against existing LLM-detection literature; which delta anchors novelty changes the story.
rationale.R1.5: Central: reframing the conceptual story entails restructuring the narrative arc—specifically whether the DiD findings serve as motivation for CrowdAudit or vice versa, and whether the normative conclusion is load-bearing or supplementary. Structural reorganization is a necessary consequence of R1.1 work here.
